MeasurementToolMetadata.Description: Karolinska Sleepiness Scale (KSS) ratings collected during three separate sessions. The KSS is a 9-point self report scale of momentary sleepiness where higher scores indicate greater sleepiness.

participant_id.Description: Unique participant ID that matches the participant_id in participants.tsv.

sess-01_kss.Description: Karolinska Sleepiness Scale rating for the participant in session 01. Higher scores indicate greater subjective sleepiness at the time of rating.

Levels.7: Sleepy, but no difficulty remaining awake
Levels.8: Sleepy, some effort needed to remain awake
Levels.9: Very sleepy, strong effort needed to remain awake, effectively fighting sleep

sess-02_kss.Description: Karolinska Sleepiness Scale rating for the participant in session 02. Higher scores indicate greater subjective sleepiness at the time of rating.

Levels.7: Sleepy, but no difficulty remaining awake
Levels.8: Sleepy, some effort needed to remain awake
Levels.9: Very sleepy, strong effort needed to remain awake, effectively fighting sleep

sess-03_kss.Description: Karolinska Sleepiness Scale rating for the participant in session 03. Higher scores indicate greater subjective sleepiness at the time of rating.

Levels.7: Sleepy, but no difficulty remaining awake
Levels.8: Sleepy, some effort needed to remain awake
Levels.9: Very sleepy, strong effort needed to remain awake, effectively fighting sleep
